data.title: Global Food Prices Rebound in February
data.description:
The FAO Food Price Index increased 1.6% to 127.1 points in February 2025, after falling 1.8% in January.

Sugar prices soared 6.6%, rebounding after three consecutive declines, amid concerns over tighter global supplies in the 2024/25 season.

Declining production prospects in India and concerns over the impact of recent dry weather on the upcoming crop in Brazil also underpinned the increase in prices.

In addition, dairy cost jumped 4% to the highest level since October 2022, driven by higher prices across all major dairy products.

Prices of vegetable oils went up 2% on higher quotations across palm, rapeseed, soy and sunflower oils and prices of cereals increased 0.7% to hit the highest level in five months.

Wheat export prices increased, driven by tighter domestic supplies in Russia, which constrained export volumes and shifted demand to other suppliers.

Meanwhile, meat prices edged down marginally by 0.1%, with international poultry meat and pig prices declining.

data.title: Patterson-UTI Energy Reports Average Number of Drilling Rigs for February
data.description:
Patterson-UTI Energy said late Thursday it had an average of 107 drilling rigs operating in the US during February.

For the two months ended Feb. 28, the company had an average of 107 drilling rigs operating in the country, it said.

data.title: Euro High-Yield Refinancing Risks Remain Low — Market Talk
data.description: Refinancing risks on euro-denominated junk bonds remain low even as eurozone sovereign yields--or 'risk-free' rates--rise, Barclays analysts say in a note. Bund yields and eurozone sovereign yields have risen considerably this week after Germany revealed fiscal expansion plans, but eurozone junk bond yields have climbed modestly by only 7 basis points to 5.5% at Wednesday's close, Barclays says. "In reality, [euro junk bond] yields are still around 15bps lower versus the start of the year and around 50bps lower versus the 2024 average," they say. Yields on euro junk bonds are less likely to move up sharply, keeping refinancing risks low, Barclays says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Surmodics Vows to Oppose FTC Decision to Block GTCR Deal
data.description:
Surmodics said late Thursday it disagrees with the US Federal Trade Commission's decision to challenge the company's proposed acquisition by private-equity firm GTCR, which owns a stake in medical coatings maker Biocoat.

The antitrust regulator has sued to block the deal alleging that it's anticompetitive.

The proposed merger would result in "a highly concentrated market for outsourced hydrophilic coatings and eliminate significant head-to-head competition" between Surmodics and Biocoat, the FTC said Thursday.

Surmodics said it was disappointed by the FTC's decision to begin litigation, adding that the deal was "pro-competitive."

"Surmodics intends to vigorously defend this case in court in order to complete the merger," the company said.

Surmodics shares were down 4.8% in recent premarket activity Friday.

data.title: Goldman Sachs ups Akzo Nobel to 'buy', citing a pivot on net pricing and volume outlook
data.description:
** Goldman Sachs raises Dulux paint maker Akzo Nobel to "buy" from "sell"

** Oversupply in chemicals industry offset anticipated raw materials cost pressure from EU tariffs, while outlook for European growth is improving, GS says

** "Following 44 months of negative consensus revisions and share price de-rating, we expect a trough to be reached during 1H25 and for Akzo Nobel to re-rate around German fiscal reform," says the broker

** Broker highlights "upside risk from pent-up demand as cyclical industries refocus on volume growth rather than price/mixing strategies"

** Restructuring measures to address inflated cost base and strategic review of South Asia deco assets will also provide upsides, says broker

** Shares rise 2.5% 0932 GMT

** Out of 21 analysts that cover Akzo Nobel, 13 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy", ​six rate "hold" and two​ rate it "strong sell" or "sell"

data.title: Far East Horizon Records 38% Lower Profit in 2024
data.description:
Far East Horizon recorded a profit attributable to shareholders of the parent of 3.86 billion yuan in 2024, 37.6% lower than 6.19 billion yuan from 2023, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The earnings per share attributable to equity holders of the parent were 0.84 yuan, compared with 1.33 yuan per share from a year earlier.

The financial services company's total revenue for the year was 37.7 billion yuan, 0.55% lower than 38.0 billion yuan from the year-ago period.

The slip in the revenue is attributable to a contraction in traditional financial business resulting from the economic downturn and lower demand. However, the inclusive finance segment saw growth while industrial operation remained stable.

The company recommended a final dividend of HK$0.30 per share for the reporting year, compared with HK$0.50 from the preceding year. The dividend will be paid on May 6 to the shareholders on record as of April 24.

data.title: Update: Broadcom Fiscal Q1 Non-GAAP Earnings, Sales Rise; Q2 Outlook Beats; Shares Up Premarket
data.description:
(Updates with the stock move in the headline and the first paragraph.)

Broadcom shares were up nearly 13% in premarket activity Friday after the company reported overnight a fiscal Q1 earnings and sales beat, while its guidance for next quarter's sales was also above consensus.

The company reported fiscal Q1 non-GAAP earnings late Thursday of $1.60 per diluted share, up from $1.10 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.51.

Revenue for the quarter ended Feb. 2 was $14.92 billion, up from $11.96 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $14.62 billion.

The company expects fiscal Q2 revenue of about $14.9 billion. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $14.71 billion.

data.description: Kao has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from 6,700 yen to 8,400 yen, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.description: Kyowa Kirin has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from 2,100 yen to 3,500 yen, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: Bulgaria Retail Sales Growth Eases in January
data.description:
Retail sales in Bulgaria rose by 8.1% year-on-year in January 2025, slowing from an upwardly revised nine-month high of 9% in December.

This marks the softest growth in three months, as sales growth for food, beverages, and tobacco sharply slowed to 3.9%, down from 9.3% in December.

Meanwhile, trade rebounded broadly for automotive fuel in specialized stores (15.5% vs. -5.6%), and sales of non-food products grew faster (11% vs. 8.8%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, retail trade grew by 0.1%, easing from a revised 0.4% gain in December.

data.title: British American Tobacco's Canadian Litigation Plan Gets Court Approval
data.description:
By Michael Susin

British American Tobacco said a Canadian court approved its plan to settle outstanding litigation in the country.

The FTSE 100 cigarette maker--which houses the Kent, Dunhill and Lucky Strike brands--on Friday said that the Ontario Superior Court of Justice approved the plan of compromise and arrangement for its Canadian subsidiary Imperial Tobacco Canada.

The company said that the plan resolves all Canadian tobacco litigation, and the settlement will be funded by its subsidiary's funds and the profits generated from the future sale of tobacco products in Canada.

It will also allow the subsidiary to emerge from its creditor protection under the Canadian Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act since 2019.

The company had made 6.2 billion-pound ($7.99 billion) provision related to the proposed settlement in Canada, according to the company's latest earnings.

The sanction doesn't impact British American Tobacco's current guidance for 2025. It continues to see around 1% of revenue growth, and adjusted profit from operations--the company's preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--growth between 1.5% and 2.5%.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Asante Net Sales Edge Down in February
data.description:
Asante reported February net sales of 991 million yen, slightly down by 0.4% from a year earlier, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

For the April 2024 to February 2025 period, the pest control and home maintenance firm recorded a year-on-year sales increase of 2.1%, with total revenue reaching 12.8 billion yen.

data.title: FTSE Straits Times Index Closes 0.1% Lower at 3914.48
Ceres Meets All Prime Market Listing Compliance
data.description:
Ceres has met all Prime Market listing maintenance standards as of Dec. 31, 2024, according to its Friday filing with the Tokyo bourse.

The company increased its number of shareholders to 10,584 and its market capitalization of tradable shares to 18.2 billion yen, exceeding listing criteria.

Efforts to comply included improving earnings per share through profit recovery, reducing capital costs via enhanced investor relations, and executing its Medium-Term Management Plan 2026.

Ceres shares shed nearly 4% at market close.
